description: Reliable browser release and stale chrome/chromium process cleanup for Pibo-managed browser pools and auth profile leases.

userStories.title: Fix stale browser process matching for chrome and chromium
userStories.description: As an operator, I want stale cleanup to detect both Chrome and Chromium processes so that worker browser leaks are not missed because of binary naming.
acceptanceCriteria: Update stale browser process matching to recognize both chrome and chromium command names
Scope process matching to Pibo-managed user-data directories, recorded pool state, or recorded pid metadata
Do not match unrelated host browser processes that lack Pibo-managed profile metadata
Add tests covering /usr/bin/chromium, google-chrome, unrelated chromium profile, and auth template profile cases
Tests pass
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: Implemented process-table based stale browser matching in the browser-use wrapper. Cleanup now recognizes chrome, google-chrome, chromium, and chromium-browser command names only when the command line includes the exact managed --user-data-dir path. Added focused wrapper tests covering /usr/bin/chromium, google-chrome, an unrelated chromium profile, and auth-template profile preservation. Evidence recorded in IMPLEMENTATION_PROGRESS.md: focused Docker-worker tests passed, real wrapper/process-table validation with actual Chromium passed, and npm run typecheck passed.

userStories.title: Terminate stale managed browser process trees safely
userStories.description: As a tooling engineer, I want cleanup to terminate the exact stale managed browser process tree so that Chromium children do not survive release or reap.
acceptanceCriteria: Reap prefers recorded pid and process group id before command-line matching
Termination uses a bounded graceful signal then a stronger fallback only for Pibo-managed browser process trees
Reap handles already-dead pids and missing pid files without failing the entire cleanup
Stale CDP pid and port files are removed only after the process is confirmed dead or killed
Focused tests cover live process group, dead pid, missing pid file, and stale CDP file cleanup
Tests pass
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: Implemented safer stale managed browser cleanup in the browser-use wrapper. Cleanup now prefers recorded browser-pool state and CDP pid metadata, uses bounded TERM then KILL for the matching managed process group when safe, falls back to exact Pibo-managed chrome/chromium user-data-dir process matching, tolerates dead or missing pid metadata, treats zombies as gone, and removes stale CDP pid/port and Singleton files only after the managed browser is confirmed gone. Added focused tests for live process-group termination, dead pid stale CDP cleanup, missing pid-file tolerance, and stale CDP file cleanup. Evidence recorded in IMPLEMENTATION_PROGRESS.md: focused Docker-worker tests passed, real Docker-worker Chromium wrapper validation passed, and npm run typecheck passed.

userStories.title: Clean browser lease tabs and contexts on release
userStories.description: As an agent, I want browser lease release to close or reset my browser state so that the next check starts from a clean context when the browser is reused.
acceptanceCriteria: Release attempts bounded CDP cleanup for lease-owned tabs or contexts when the managed browser is reachable
Release updates active lease counters and last-used timestamps even when CDP cleanup fails
Release records cleanup status and last error for diagnostics
Tests cover successful CDP cleanup, CDP timeout/failure, and release after browser process exit
Tests pass
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: Implemented bounded CDP tab cleanup on browser-pool lease release; release clears active lease state/counters and records cleanupStatus/lastError for success, CDP failure, and exited-browser paths. Evidence recorded in IMPLEMENTATION_PROGRESS.md.

userStories.title: Coordinate authenticated profile leases with browser pool leases
userStories.description: As an auth workflow user, I want releasing an authenticated browser-use slot to also release the matching managed browser lease without touching the reusable auth template.
acceptanceCriteria: Auth lease acquire records or returns the associated browser-pool lease id when managed-pool mode is active
Auth lease release releases the associated browser-pool lease when present
Auth lease reap-stale terminates known managed browser processes for expired slots when safe
Cleanup never deletes or kills the auth template profile unless an explicit auth-template workflow targets it
Tests cover auth slot release, expired auth slot reap, missing browser-pool lease, and template preservation
Tests pass
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: Implemented auth lease/browser-pool coordination. Auth lease acquire now records/returns browserPoolLeaseId and exports PIBO_BROWSER_POOL_LEASE_ID; release calls browser-pool release when an associated managed pool lease is present; reap-stale safely terminates the matching managed browser process only when the pool state active lease id and user-data dir match the expired auth slot, preserving the auth template. Evidence recorded in IMPLEMENTATION_PROGRESS.md: focused Docker-worker CLI tests passed and npm run typecheck passed.

userStories.title: Add idle browser pool reaping and cleanup reporting
userStories.description: As an operator, I want idle managed browsers to recycle automatically or manually so that long-lived workers do not keep Chromium running forever.
acceptanceCriteria: Implement idle reap behavior for pools with no active lease and last-used time older than the configured idle timeout
Reap output reports affected leases, browser pools, process trees, and stale state files
Text output includes safe next commands when cleanup fails or a worker is marked dirty
JSON output includes counts and per-pool cleanup results
Tests cover idle eligible pool, active non-eligible pool, stale process cleanup, and dirty cleanup failure state
Tests pass
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: Implemented idle browser-pool reaping and cleanup reporting. Added a locked reap API that skips active leases, reaps no-active idle/stale/dirty pools, terminates managed process trees using recorded pid/process-group metadata, removes stale browser state files, clears reaped pool state to empty, and marks cleanup failures dirty with diagnostics. Added `pibo tools browser-use pool reap` text/JSON output with affected lease, pool, process-tree, and stale-file counts plus safe next commands on dirty failures. Evidence recorded in IMPLEMENTATION_PROGRESS.md: focused Docker-worker tests passed, real Docker-worker process-tree reap validation passed, and npm run typecheck passed.
